key_findings: INSERT OR IGNORE in persistence_gateway.py insert_event() makes persistence re-runs naturally idempotent — FM-04 drill proves this with before/after COUNT(*) comparison
The most severe data loss risk is FM-17 (WAL corruption): maximum loss is one ingest_file() call worth of uncommitted events. All other drills produce zero data loss.
ClickHouse health check must gate batch claiming — during outage, rows must stay PENDING (not FAILED) to avoid false retry_count increments and spurious DLQ escalation
All recovery mechanisms already exist in WO-00022/WO-00023 infrastructure: lease reaper, claim reaper, outbox retry, DLQ promotion. Drills verify these work correctly end-to-end.
MTTR < 15 min is achievable for all automated recovery drills. Manual intervention drills (FM-08, FM-10) depend on operator following DATABASE_OPS.md runbooks.
FM-20 (concurrent load drill) is the most important pre-production drill — it validates the WAL mode concurrent access assumption and hourly report monotonicity under realistic conditions.

decisions.decision: Use SIGKILL (not SIGTERM) for all process kill drills
decisions.rationale: SIGKILL simulates OOM/kernel kill — the actual failure mode in production; SIGTERM with graceful cleanup would not exercise the reaper/recovery path

decisions.decision: Pre/post snapshot queries run inside SQLite (no external tools)
decisions.rationale: Self-contained harness; SQLite is the single source of truth; snapshot queries are the same queries used in WO-00024 hourly report

decisions.decision: FM-17 (WAL corruption) runs on a copy of the DB
decisions.rationale: Truncating WAL is irreversible on the original DB; drill validates SQLite durability semantics without production risk

decisions.decision: 20 drills organized in 6 sections with independent pass/fail gates
decisions.rationale: Allows partial rollout approval (e.g., proceed with ClickHouse sink while Neo4j drill is pending); clear mandatory vs advisory classification

risk_model.risk: FM-17 WAL corruption drill damages production DB
CH health check gate not implemented in sink worker — rows marked FAILED during outage
risk_model.mitigation: WO-00028 spec includes explicit health check before batch claim; FM-08 verifies this behavior

risk_model.risk: FM-20 concurrent drill exposes SQLITE_BUSY under high contention
risk_model.mitigation: WAL mode supports concurrent reads + one writer; BEGIN IMMEDIATE serializes write attempts; if SQLITE_BUSY observed, increase SQLite timeout parameter

recommended_actions: Run Section A + B drills first (scanner + persistence) — these are infrastructure prerequisites for all other drills
Run FM-08 (CH outage) before any ClickHouse sink deployment to verify health-check gate behavior
Run FM-19 (replay idempotency) as a regression drill after every schema or worker change
Run FM-20 (concurrent load) as the final gate before production rollout

cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00022: chunk_queue + reclaim_stale_leases() required for Section A drills
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00023: sink_outbox + dead_letter_events required for Section C/D drills
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00024: ALT-03 alert fired in FM-10 drill; ALT-02 alert fired in FM-06 drill
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00028: clickhouse_sink_worker.py CLAIMED status + reaper required for FM-09
